properties.remark: Photo of a quarter sized hailstone next to a ruler sent in via social media.
Large tree down with a picture of where it clipped a house damaging the roof and gutters. Also posted a video of frothy lake as the storm passed. From Facebook.
Tree down at Gypsy Creek Farm.
Tree down on Munger Shaw Road.
Large red pine tree on house.
Pea Size hail and heavy wind around 605 pm blew over a 40 pound deck chair with a steel base and branches around 1.5 inches in diameter came off a few trees.
Report sent in via social media with a photo.
Report sent in via social media. Time estimated from radar.
Public estimated hail the size of a quarter on the southwest side of Lac Sault Dore.
Public report of hail ranging from Quarter to golf ball sized hail on Pine Lake in central Iron County.

Report of a huge and healthy, but old, maple tree that snapped over a powerline 0.5 miles SW of this location, and a 2 ft in diameter pine that had fallen over CR135 about 1 mile to the east that was uprooted.

Several 18 to 20 inch in diameter aspen trees down. About half of them had roots showing and the other half were broken some place half way or lower on the trunk. There were a few trees that had about the top third or higher of the trunk broken off. All of the damage limbs or trunks were on the north side of the tree base.

There was a report of a road that washed out less than 1 mile to the north of this location around 7 PM, and other washouts along CR135 near CR12 around 830 PM, but water was receding at that time. Locations are approximate. Report received via email.

Six delayed reports of trees down on roadways especially County Highway H along Scenic Drive and on Yderstad Road and related driveways between the Village of Delta and the town of Drummond in central Bayfield County per Sheriffs office dispatch. Time estimated based on radar and per dispatch call reports tracking locations of the downed trees along the noted track.
Picture via social media of a large pine tree fallen across 5th Avenue in Virginia. Time estimated from radar.

Delayed report from a trained spotter of 4 trees laying on Highway GG between Clam Lake and Mellen in central Ashland County. Time given by Trained Spotter and verified with radar imagery. Location estimated based on radar imagery.
Delayed report. Time estimated from radar.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 4 S Superior. Public social media report with photo showing uprooted tree about 2 foot diameter laying on the ground in the Village of Superior near WI Hwy 105 and Hwy 35. Time estimated based on radar imagery and delayed report timing.

A photo of a tree fallen down over a fence in the north side of Cloquet area was shared via Facebook. The tree was large enough to cause damage to the fence upon falling, per the picture, which showed a few wooden fence pieces dismantled. The tree appeared healthy. Location is approximate.

Delayed report. Public social media report with photo showing a partially dead or at least sick tree snapped at the trunk about 10 feet from the base right in the town of Iron River. Time estimated from radar.
Tree on powerline. Winds made it feel like their car was going to be blown off the road.

Pictures of many trees down throughout a campground. These all appear to be large and healthy trees that were uprooted due to strong thunderstorm winds. These pictures were seen on Facebook. Location is approximate. Time estimated by radar.

Hwy 1 between Tower and Cook had spots with water across the road and a spot with only one lane opened. There is also a spot on Hwy 22 that has water flowing across it. This spot is deeper. Observer lost power to their vehicle after hitting it.
Streets and yards in the city of Cook underwater. Low spots of Highway 24 underwater. Up to 6 to 12 inches of water across roads.
